title: Meet the article
description: Identify articles and the words they describe.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which word in the sentence is an article?**

>The guitar was missing strings.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific.

_The_, _a_, and _an_ are the only articles in English.
The only article in this sentence is **"the."**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which word in the sentence is an article?**

>Take the next exit for Legoland.

[[☃ radio 1]]

Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific.

_The_, _a_, and _an_ are the only articles in English.
The only article in this sentence is **"the."**

assessment_items.question_content:
Which word does **the bolded article** refer to?

>I wore **a** green dress.

Which word or phrase does **a** describe?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific. They come before the noun they describe.
In this sentence, "a" describes the noun **"dress"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which word does **the bolded article** refer to?

>Noelle was **the** first person to arrive.

Which word or phrase does **the** refer to?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific. They come before the noun they describe.
In this sentence, "the" describes the noun **"person"**.

assessment_items.question_content:

Which word or phrase does **the** refer to?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Articles are a type of adjectives that tells you whether or not something is specific. They come before the noun they describe.
In this sentence, "the" describes the noun **"cows"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
